providers.name: Graphify
providers.usage_note: Confirmed setup installs the Graphify CLI and current-host project skill, resolves `graphify` from the original PATH or provider-standard user tool path, generates project-root `graphify-out/` by trying `graphify extract .` and falling back to code-only `graphify update .` when semantic extract cannot run, then runs one bounded `graphify update . --force` repair only if Graphify refuses overwrite and suggests `--force`. It then enables provider-native `graphify hook install` when the target is a git repo. If the resolved Graphify CLI is off the original PATH, setup may add a marked project-local PATH repair block to Graphify-installed hook files before verifying hook status. Only the CLI/project skill/hooks are required downstream; Graphify MCP/watch stay opt-in outside Runtime Setup. Use `$graphify .`/`/graphify .` or the resolved Graphify CLI query/path/explain for project-graph candidates, then confirm conclusions from source/test/log evidence.

commands_display.macos: uv tool install {{package}}=={{version}}; resolve graphify from PATH or $HOME/.local/bin/graphify; graphify install --project --platform <current-host>; graphify extract . || graphify update .; graphify update . --force only after provider overwrite refusal; graphify hook install
commands_display.linux: uv tool install {{package}}=={{version}}; resolve graphify from PATH or $HOME/.local/bin/graphify; graphify install --project --platform <current-host>; graphify extract . || graphify update .; graphify update . --force only after provider overwrite refusal; graphify hook install
commands_display.windows: uv tool install {{package}}=={{version}}; resolve graphify from PATH or $HOME/.local/bin/graphify(.exe/.cmd); graphify install --project --platform <current-host>; graphify extract . || graphify update .; graphify update . --force only after provider overwrite refusal; graphify hook install

installation.next_action: Run the current host setup workflow and confirm the CodeGraph/Graphify provider pack, or use the current host subset entrypoint (`/spec:mcp-setup --only graphify` on Claude, `$spec-mcp-setup --only graphify` on Codex) for explicit install-init. The {{package}} version pin is {{version}}.

safety.install_effect: Installs PyPI package `{{package}}=={{version}}`, resolves `graphify` from the user's original PATH or provider-standard user tool path, and verifies the resolved CLI. Confirmed provider-pack setup runs current-host project skill install such as `graphify install --project --platform codex`, normalizes the provider-written `AGENTS.md`/`CLAUDE.md` `## graphify` instruction section to resolved CLI/manual-visibility/direct-source-fallback wording, generates project-root `graphify-out/` via `graphify extract .` with a provider-native `graphify update .` code-only fallback, runs one bounded `graphify update . --force` repair only after Graphify emits overwrite refusal plus a force hint, probes query/hook commands through the resolved CLI, installs project-level `graphify hook install` in git repos, and may add a marked project-local PATH repair block to Graphify-installed hook files before `graphify hook status` when the resolved CLI is off the original PATH. Provider writes may include `.codex/skills/graphify/`, `.codex/hooks.json`, `AGENTS.md`, `.claude/skills/graphify/`, `CLAUDE.md`, `graphify-out/`, and `.git/hooks/*`; setup reports them as provider-owned runtime/project output, does not edit shell profiles, does not vendor the Graphify skill, and does not auto-add, auto-commit, or promote provider output to spec-first source.
